title: fix issue #791 - modify a cell style on existing excel file may affec…
author: DantSu

comments.author: DantSu
comments.body: I haven't update the style section of README file. Do I have to do this ?

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
@DantSu Thank you for contributing ExcelJS, it is always welcome for us :)
However I suppose that this PR do not resolve any problem in current logic, it is just adding new function with walk-around . Behaviour of `cell.font` and `cell.border` will stay still unpredictable when it works on read file.

comments.author: DantSu
comments.body: What do you suggest to fix this issue ?

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
I suggest that, style object should be immutable - any change on `cell.font`, `cell.border`, `cell.style` should make copy with new value set. Like `{...style, font:newFont}`, however it may has negative impact for perfomance (needs to check it).

Second possibility is a cloning style object (and its members) during reading file - probably has also negative impact for performance, but only on file read

comments.author: DantSu
comments.body: Immutable style object is definitely the best way. But it's a breaking change. The current documentation is based on mutable object.

comments.author: Siemienik
comments.body:
> Immutable style object is definitely the best way. But it's a breaking change. The current documentation is based on mutable object.

true :/

comments.author: darron1217
comments.body:
This is just adding a helper function but it may help us a lot.

How about merging it as workaround and then create another Issue/PR to apply `immutable` gradually?
